title: Itemized Bill Request
description: Generate a letter requesting a detailed itemized bill from your healthcare provider

why_itemize.title: Why request an itemized bill?
why_itemize.body:
The bill you received is probably a summary. It shows a total but not the individual charges. An itemized bill breaks down every single service, procedure, and supply with its billing code and price.

This is your most powerful tool because:

• 49-80% of medical bills contain errors
• The average hospital bill over $10,000 has about $1,300 in overcharges
• You can't dispute what you can't see

callout.text: Requesting an itemized bill is free, it's your right, and many bills drop in price just from asking, because providers know errors will be found.

collect_provider_info.subtext: Enter the name and address from the bill you received. This is who we'll address the letter to.

fields.label: Provider / Hospital name
fields.placeholder: e.g., General Hospital, Dr. Smith's Office

validation.message: Please enter a valid ZIP code (e.g., 12345 or 12345-6789)

collect_bill_details.subtext: You can find this information on the bill you received. If you don't have all the details, fill in what you can.

fields.label: Account or reference number
fields.placeholder: Usually at the top of your bill

collect_sender_info.subtext: This is who the letter is from. Your name and address will appear at the top of the letter.

meta.helpText: We need your name and address so the provider can identify your account and respond to you. This information stays in your browser.

fields.label: Your full name
fields.placeholder: As it appears on your medical records

validation.message: Please enter a valid ZIP code

already_itemized.prompt: Have you already received an itemized bill?
already_itemized.subtext: An itemized bill lists each individual charge with a billing code (like CPT or HCPCS). A summary bill just shows a total.

options.label: No, I only have a summary bill
Yes, I already have an itemized bill
options.helpText: Great! You may want to review it for errors instead.

options.label: I'm not sure
options.helpText: If it only shows a total amount without individual line items, it's a summary.

already_have_tip.title: Good, you're ahead of the game
already_have_tip.body:
Since you already have an itemized bill, here are things to look for:

• Duplicate charges (same service billed twice)
• Services you don't remember receiving
• Charges that seem unreasonably high
• Operating room time that seems too long
• Medications you weren't given

If you find anything suspicious, you may want to use our other tools to dispute those specific charges.

callout.text: You can still generate the request letter. It's useful to have a formal record that you've requested full documentation.

in_collections.prompt: Has this bill been sent to a collections agency?
in_collections.subtext: If a collections agency has contacted you (not the original provider), your situation requires different protections.

options.label: No, it's still with the original provider
Yes, a collector has contacted me
I'm not sure
options.helpText: If the bill came from a company different than where you received care, it may be in collections.

collections_warning.title: Important: bills in collections need additional action
collections_warning.body:
When a bill is in collections, you have additional rights under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA). We'll generate your itemized bill request, but you should also consider filing a debt validation demand with the collector.

After you finish here, we recommend using our Debt Validation tool.

callout.text: You have 30 days from receiving the collector's written notice to demand debt validation. If that window is still open, act quickly.

nextSteps.text: Print this letter and send it via certified mail with return receipt requested
nextSteps.details: Certified mail creates a legal record that the provider received your request. Keep the receipt.
Save a copy of the letter, the certified mail receipt, and the original bill you received.

nextSteps.text: Wait for the itemized bill (allow 30 days)
nextSteps.details: Most providers respond within 2-4 weeks. If they don't respond within 30 days, you have grounds to file a complaint.

nextSteps.text: Review the itemized bill carefully when it arrives
nextSteps.details: Look for duplicate charges, services you don't remember, and charges that seem unreasonably high. Our Playbook guide on itemized bills can help.

nextSteps.text: If they don't respond, file a complaint with your state Attorney General
nextSteps.details: Failure to provide an itemized bill when requested is a violation of patient rights in most states.
